title: A Federal Judge Just Killed Trump's $100,000 H-1B Visa Tax
script: Unconstitutional! On June 8, U.S. District Judge Leo Sorokin in Boston struck down President Trump's $100,000 supplementary fee on new H-1B visas for skilled workers. Sorokin ruled the charge violated the Administrative Procedure Act — the Constitution gives only Congress the power to tax, not the executive branch. A coalition of 20 state attorneys general had sued to block it. Employers applying for H-1B workers had been forced to pay the $100,000 surcharge on top of standard filing fees of $2,000 to $5,000. The ruling takes effect immediately. The tech industry, which relies heavily on H-1B visas to hire engineers, breathed a collective sigh of relief. Should Congress get to decide who pays what to bring in a foreign worker?
